criteriaCoverage.evidence:
full-lifecycle.integration (real PG): trigger->wait->sweep deferral->condition->action(send)->exit across ticks; completed terminal, one outbox dedupe_key campaign::

:a. campaign-journey cross-phase repeats via real enrollFromSegmentChange + runEnrollment + real dispatchOutbox + §8 compiler, messages_log written.

wait-deferral.integration: next_run_at=now+2h, sweep at +1h returns nothing, +2h01s returns row. branch-routing.integration via §8 buildSegmentMatch (workspace_id $1 + AND p.id=$): purchase=3 onTrue gets send, purchase=0 onFalse no send; one outbox for matching profile.

CAS on updated_at::text (round-trips microsecond precision; ms guard would never re-match). idempotent-concurrent-sweep: 10 concurrent runEnrollment Promise.all shared pool on same due enrollment -> EXACTLY 1 completed + 9 skipped, EXACTLY 1 outbox, status completed (non-vacuous, broken CAS fails it). Double-guarded by outbox dedupe_key UNIQUE.

action-send-through-dispatcher uses REAL dispatchOutbox: suppressed skipped (SES not called), unverified workspace refused (ses.sends 0). grep run/core/enroll/handler/deps: NO SES usage. enrollment-from-segment-entry: entered enrolls, second entered no-op (once via UNIQUE+ON CONFLICT), exited none. workspace-scoping: WS_A entry no bleed to WS_B, runStatementsInWorkspaceTx refuses cross-workspace stmt. Every write workspace_id $1; sweep loads workspace_id from row.

recommendations: Branch test uses purchase>=1 (3 vs 0); add a boundary case at the exact threshold to harden the comparator.
Add a comment noting the CAS claim is not part of the advance tx (only the guard token links them).
handler.ts swallows per-row errors silently; add structured logging so a persistently-failing enrollment is observable (only parks 'failed' on MAX_STEPS, not transient errors).
